name: Dromana Estate
signature: One of the Peninsula's longer-standing names  -  cool-climate classics alongside a small run of Italian-leaning bottlings
editorNote:
Dromana Estate is one of the Peninsula's founding names and has quietly reinvented itself more than once across its history. The current cellar door pours a line-up that leans on the regional classics  -  Chardonnay, Pinot Noir  -  alongside a small run of Italian-leaning wines the team has been building out over the past few years.

The estate sits on the quieter, eastern side of the Peninsula wine country, which makes it a genuinely useful alternative when the flagship Red Hill cellar doors are mid-long-weekend bedlam. The tasting counter is unpretentious, the staff are knowledgeable, and the prices sit slightly below the ridge-top average without loss of quality.

Treat Dromana Estate as your decompression stop after a busy morning up on the ridge, or as your first stop on a quieter midweek cellar-door day.
